spots.name: Hội An Ancient Town
spots.shortDescription: A lantern-lit riverside trading town with preserved timber houses, temples and Japanese-influenced bridges.
spots.whyVisit: Atmospheric evenings, cycling lanes, cooking classes and heritage architecture.
spots.openingHours: Old town is accessible daily; ticketed heritage sites have daytime hours
spots.entranceFee: Old Town sightseeing ticket required for many heritage sites
spots.topActivities: Lantern walk, cycling, cooking class, market visit
spots.nearbyAttractions: An Bàng Beach, My Son Sanctuary
spots.travelTips: Explore early morning or after sunset, and carry small cash for food stalls.

spots.name: Tràng An Scenic Landscape Complex
spots.shortDescription: A UNESCO-listed landscape of limestone karsts, waterways, caves and ancient temples.
spots.whyVisit: Rowboat journeys through cave tunnels and dramatic ‘land Ha Long Bay’ scenery.
spots.entranceFee: Boat-route ticket required; check current local price
spots.topActivities: Sampan boat ride, cave passage, temple stops
spots.nearbyAttractions: Tam Cốc, Hoa Lư Ancient Capital
spots.travelTips: Go early to avoid crowds and bring sun protection for the open boat.
spots.mapQuery: Trang An Scenic Landscape Complex, Ninh Binh, Vietnam

spots.name: Phong Nha–Kẻ Bàng National Park
spots.shortDescription: A dramatic karst national park celebrated for huge cave systems, rivers and jungle landscapes.
spots.whyVisit: Cave excursions, river trips and some of Asia’s most spectacular underground scenery.
spots.openingHours: Tours operate in daylight; weather affects access
spots.topActivities: Phong Nha Cave boat tour, Paradise Cave, jungle trekking
spots.travelTips: Book specialist caves well ahead and avoid the wettest months for more reliable access.
spots.mapQuery: Phong Nha Ke Bang National Park, Vietnam

spots.name: Ho Chi Minh City
spots.shortDescription: Vietnam’s energetic southern metropolis, mixing colonial landmarks, markets, museums and modern cafés.
spots.whyVisit: Food, history, local markets and a fast-moving urban atmosphere.
spots.entranceFee: Most public areas free; museums are ticketed
spots.topActivities: Ben Thanh Market, War Remnants Museum, street-food tour
spots.travelTips: Use ride-hailing for longer trips and plan indoor museum visits during the hottest part of the day.

spots.name: George Town Heritage Zone
spots.shortDescription: A UNESCO-listed port city of colourful shophouses, clan houses, temples and celebrated street food.
spots.whyVisit: Living heritage, mural walks, Peranakan culture and Malaysia’s most famous food scene.
spots.openingHours: Historic streets are always accessible; sites vary
spots.entranceFee: Public streets free; museums and mansions ticketed
spots.topActivities: Street-art walk, food tour, clan jetties, museum visits
spots.nearbyAttractions: Cheong Fatt Tze Mansion, Kek Lok Si Temple
spots.travelTips: Walk early or late for cooler weather and use a map to find murals beyond the busiest lanes.

spots.name: Melaka Historic City
spots.shortDescription: A UNESCO-recognised Straits trading city shaped by Malay, Chinese, Portuguese, Dutch and British heritage.
spots.whyVisit: Riverside walks, Peranakan culture, historic squares and night-market snacks.
spots.openingHours: Historic district accessible daily; individual sites vary
spots.topActivities: Jonker Walk, river cruise, Stadthuys, Peranakan food
spots.travelTips: Visit Jonker Street on a weekend evening for the night market, but expect crowds.

spots.name: Langkawi UNESCO Global Geopark
spots.shortDescription: An island archipelago of beaches, mangroves, limestone formations and rainforest landscapes.
spots.whyVisit: Island-hopping, cable-car views, mangrove tours and relaxed beach time.
spots.entranceFee: Island access free; tours and cable car ticketed
spots.topActivities: SkyCab, Kilim mangrove tour, beach hopping
spots.travelTips: Book boat tours with licensed operators and plan outdoor activities before afternoon rain.

spots.name: Batu Caves
spots.shortDescription: A dramatic limestone cave complex with Hindu shrines and the towering golden Murugan statue.
spots.whyVisit: Temple culture, colourful steps and one of Kuala Lumpur’s most memorable landmarks.
spots.entranceFee: Main cave access generally free; some caves ticketed
spots.topActivities: Temple visit, stair climb, cave photography
spots.travelTips: Dress modestly, watch for monkeys and bring water for the stairs.

spots.name: Ha Long Bay
spots.shortDescription: A UNESCO World Heritage seascape of limestone islands rising from emerald water.
spots.whyVisit: Cruises, kayaking, caves and wide coastal views.
spots.nearbyAttractions: Ti Top Island, Sung Sot Cave
spots.travelTips: Book a cruise ahead, bring sunscreen and check weather conditions.
spots.mapQuery: Ha Long Bay, Quang Ninh, Vietnam

spots.name: Gardens by the Bay
spots.shortDescription: A futuristic waterfront garden known for its Supertree Grove and cooled conservatories.
spots.whyVisit: Cloud forests, floral displays, skyline views and evening light shows.
spots.openingHours: Outdoor gardens daily; conservatory hours vary
spots.topActivities: Supertree light show, Cloud Forest, Flower Dome
spots.travelTips: Book conservatory tickets online and stay for the evening light show.

spots.name: The Grand Palace
spots.shortDescription: Bangkok’s dazzling former royal complex, home to the sacred Emerald Buddha.
spots.whyVisit: Thai architecture, gilded temples and royal history.
spots.topActivities: Temple photography, Wat Phra Kaew, royal architecture
spots.travelTips: Dress modestly, arrive early and ignore unofficial ticket touts.

spots.name: Mount Fuji
spots.shortDescription: Japan’s iconic volcanic peak, admired for its near-perfect cone and seasonal landscapes.
spots.whyVisit: Classic views, hiking, lakeside scenery and Japanese cultural symbolism.
spots.bestTime: October–February for clear views; July–September for climbing
spots.entranceFee: Viewing areas vary; climbing rules and fees vary
spots.travelTips: Check visibility forecasts and trail status before travelling.

spots.name: Petronas Twin Towers
spots.shortDescription: The twin towers define Kuala Lumpur’s skyline with an elegant steel-and-glass design.
spots.whyVisit: Skybridge views, observation deck and the surrounding KLCC district.
spots.travelTips: Reserve timed entry online, especially on weekends.
spots.mapQuery: Petronas Twin Towers, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

spots.name: Angkor Wat
spots.shortDescription: The world’s largest religious monument and the centrepiece of Angkor’s temple complex.
spots.whyVisit: Sunrise, intricate bas-reliefs and monumental Khmer architecture.
spots.travelTips: Buy your Angkor pass in advance and carry water in the heat.

spots.name: Borobudur Temple
spots.shortDescription: A vast ninth-century Buddhist monument of stone terraces and bell-shaped stupas.
spots.whyVisit: Sunrise atmosphere, Buddhist art and views over volcanic Central Java.
spots.openingHours: Morning to afternoon; sunrise access varies
spots.travelTips: Wear comfortable shoes and book sunrise access early if offered.

spots.name: Chocolate Hills
spots.shortDescription: More than a thousand rounded limestone hills that turn cocoa-brown in the dry season.
spots.whyVisit: A uniquely Philippine landscape with broad viewing-deck panoramas.
spots.travelTips: Visit early for cooler temperatures and clearer views.

spots.name: Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien Mosque
spots.shortDescription: A striking white-marble mosque with golden domes and a ceremonial lagoon setting.
spots.whyVisit: Brunei’s signature architecture, serene gardens and sunset photographs.
spots.openingHours: Visitor access varies around prayer times
spots.travelTips: Dress respectfully and check visiting hours around prayers.
spots.mapQuery: Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien Mosque, Brunei

spots.name: Bagan Archaeological Zone
spots.shortDescription: An immense plain scattered with thousands of Buddhist temples and pagodas.
spots.whyVisit: Sunrise temple views, ancient architecture and e-bike exploration.
spots.openingHours: Daylight hours; access conditions can vary
spots.topActivities: E-bike tour, sunrise viewing, temple exploration
spots.travelTips: Check current local travel guidance before making plans.

spots.name: Kuang Si Falls
spots.shortDescription: Tiered turquoise pools and cascades set in lush forest outside Luang Prabang.
spots.whyVisit: Swimming pools, forest walks and an easy nature escape from the old town.
spots.nearbyAttractions: Luang Prabang Old Town, Tat Kuang Si Bear Rescue Centre
spots.travelTips: Bring swimwear, a towel and shoes with grip.
spots.mapQuery: Kuang Si Falls, Luang Prabang, Laos
